# Cirth v0.4.0

Cirth v0.4.0 is a design-system cleanup release: the public CSS is more
stable across viewport sizes, controls hit the 44px target height, the
theme surface is smaller and clearer, and the docs have been rebuilt around
the current brand and contribution flow.

## Highlights

- Fixed the root font size at `100%` across breakpoints. Body text and
  controls no longer grow with the viewport; headings now scale fluidly
  with `clamp()`.
- Reworked default spacing, radii, typography, button states, table number
  rendering, and form/search spacing so values land on the Cirth token
  scale.
- Kept the WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility pass intact: focus visibility,
  contrast-sensitive component borders, control targets, and reduced-motion
  behavior remain verified from source.
- Flattened the theme source into `src/theme/`, extracted color scales,
  and replaced the old jade/slate theme matrix with optional `cobalt` and
  `coral` token presets.
- Redesigned the docs site and README around the new brand mark, with new
  Brand, Contributions, and Examples pages.

## Breaking Changes

- Removed responsive `root-font-size` entries from `$breakpoints`.
- Removed duplicate/legacy font-family custom properties:
  `--cirth-font-family-sans-serif`, `--cirth-font-family-monospace`, and
  `--cirth-font-family-ui`.
- Removed unwired `--cirth-shadow-*` and legacy `--cirth-letter-spacing-*`
  scale tokens.
- Removed jade/slate theme builds and all old per-theme build variants.
  Load the default build plus `dist/presets/cobalt.min.css` or
  `dist/presets/coral.min.css` instead.
- Renamed the underlying Sass primary color scale from `$azure-*` to
  `$amber-*`. Public semantic CSS variables such as `--cirth-primary`
  keep their names.
- Flattened internal theme source paths. SCSS remains repository source,
  not a published Sass API, but source patchers should update imports.

## CDN

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@cirthcss/cirth@0.4.0/dist/cirth.min.css">
```
